mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and consequently S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rate and declining housing starts indicates a tightening monetary policy severely impacting interest-sensitive sectors like housing, which then spills over to broader economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25 percentage points AND HOUST declines by > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises and HOUST declines as specified, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, translating into higher corporate output and profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon, particularly during economic expansions.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% MoM during an expansionary regime, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, forcing central banks to adopt restrictive policies, which ultimately leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently shows high inflation (e.g., >3% YoY) but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in real GDP growth indicates weakening aggregate demand and overall economic activity, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY growth falls below 1.5%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust employment growth combined with strong retail sales indicates a healthy and confident consumer base with increasing disposable income, which directly fuels corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 150,000 AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.7%
failure_condition: If PAYEMS and RSAFS show combined strength as specified, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon, especially in low-rate environments.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5% for 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.
